topic: Eclipse magnetic ensemble (39 eclipses) — post-c13 Chapman 1919 propagation within WIN-068
our_argument_summary: WIN-068 cites Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET ensemble (1991–2016) as confirmed prediction. Our verdict remains Std Model Explains — Chapman–Sq framework (Chapman 1919 foundational + Rastogi 1962 eclipse-specific + Yamazaki & Maute 2021 modern conductivity) plus the Kim & Chang ensemble predict the signal magnitude, geometry, and latitude dependence without invoking dome parameters. FSF geometry is explicitly routed to WIN-010/025 where it enters the predicted signal. The 39-eclipse ensemble itself does not invoke FSF. Triple-counting critique (WIN-010 BOU 2017 + WIN-025 2024 9-station + WIN-068 1991–2016 ensemble = same Sq mechanism at three aggregation levels) and post-hoc critique (Kim & Chang 2018 data is 8 years older than V51.0 incorporation) intact.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No WIN-068 domain in monitor.py. The 39-eclipse ensemble is a retrospective literature citation — no live validation logic, no API call, no comparison against a dome-derived numerical prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: monitoring = none. WIN entry exists in the published list but the automation does not touch it. Eclipse-related code in monitor.py targets the 2026 forward projection (WIN-010 lineage), not the 1991–2016 retrospective ensemble that WIN-068 actually counts.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: N/A — no validation logic to escape from.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our claim that monitor.py has no validation for WIN-068 remains accurate; grep for 068/eclipse/39-eclipse in monitor.py returns no signal-amplitude comparison code.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No 39-eclipse ensemble domain block in monitor.py — only the 2026 forward-projection lineage for WIN-010/025 appears.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The phenomenon (Y-component increase, X/Z/F decrease, 30–50° magnetic-latitude peak, ~180-minute window) is explained by Chapman–Sq (Chapman 1919 Phil. Trans. A 218; Rastogi 1962 eclipse application; Yamazaki & Maute 2021 modern conductivity). Dome relabels the cause (cavity geometry) without changing the numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Kim & Chang (2018, Adv. Space Res. 61:2040–2054) published ~8 years before V51.0 incorporation (early 2026). No pre-registration of expected values in a venue independent of the author has been located.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Ensemble signal amplitude is extracted from the 1991–2016 INTERMAGNET dataset itself. FSF (which IS dome geometry per the c12 retraction) does not enter this WIN — it is a spectator on the 39-eclipse ensemble and only relevant to the WIN-010/025 2026 forward projection.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN cluster Chapman-year inconsistency. WIN-068 was successfully propagated in c13 patches (commit 5fe67b5): all four Chapman-mentioning fields (finding, detail_evidence ¶3, detail_verdict_text ¶1, tldr_verdict) now correctly cite "Chapman (1919)" with "Rastogi (1962)" and "Yamazaki & Maute (2021)". However WIN-010 still contains 6 occurrences of "Chapman 1933" / "Chapman (1933)" (finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_verdict, tldr_evidence) and WIN-025 contains 6 occurrences likewise — neither uses Chapman 1919, Rastogi, or Yamazaki & Maute anywhere. Since WIN-068's detail_evidence ¶4 and detail_verdict_text ¶5 explicitly link to WIN-010 and WIN-025 via #win010 / #win025 anchors ("See WIN-010 and WIN-025, where FSF actually enters the predicted signal"), a reader following those links from WIN-068 will see the same mechanism attributed to Chapman 1919 in WIN-068 and Chapman 1933 in WIN-010/025. The c13 moderate (intra-WIN inconsistency) has been upgraded in scope: the bug moved from three fields of one WIN to six fields each of two sibling WINs, and is now visible on the cross-reference path the WIN-068 text explicitly invites readers to follow. Defender wedge is now stronger than the c13 version, because the cluster-level disagreement cannot be attributed to "drift within a panel" — it is structural mis-coordination between related WINs. ISS-1492 already tracks this cross-cluster issue and is assigned to EXP-241 (status: assigned-analyst), and ISS-1441/ISS-1491 are also folded into EXP-241. So this finding is a REDISCOVERY confirming the ISS-1492 bug is now worse post-c13 (because WIN-068 being correct widens the gap to its siblings), not a new issue. Do NOT open a duplicate issue — log as c14-reconfirms-ISS-1492 and recommend the decider escalate EXP-241 integration priority.
holes_found.recommendation: Integrate EXP-241 as the next decider priority. Propagate Chapman 1919 / Rastogi 1962 / Yamazaki & Maute 2021 canonical attribution to all 6 Chapman-mentioning fields in WIN-010 and all 6 in WIN-025 in a single coordinated commit. Until integrated, the cluster shows public incoherence on the exact citation the c12/c13 Chapman campaign was supposed to fix. If EXP-241 integration is blocked, consider writing a short advisory note (e.g., comment in the issue) indicating the cluster state is now maximally-visible-inconsistent and the window for fixing it without readers noticing is closing.

holes_found.description: Rastogi (1962) in the finding remains under-attributed. The c13 stronger_argument ("family reference rather than a specific paper; Rastogi published eclipse-Sq work in multiple 1962-era venues including the 15 Feb 1961 and 5 Feb 1962 Peru electrojet eclipses") is still live post-c13 patches. The finding now reads "eclipse-specific application in Rastogi 1962" without a venue, and detail_evidence ¶3 likewise says "documented by Rastogi (1962)" bare. Defender wedge: "which Rastogi paper? Different 1962 eclipse observations are not interchangeable." Fix effort is small (one or two words adding journal or DOI) and should ride on the EXP-241 cluster patch rather than be done standalone.
holes_found.recommendation: When EXP-241 propagates Rastogi across the cluster, attach a full journal/DOI (e.g., "Rastogi (1962) J. Atmos. Terr. Phys. 24:xx–yy, DOI:...") or replace with a less ambiguous modern review that cites Rastogi as a specific paper. Do not land the propagation without pinning Rastogi — otherwise we propagate the ambiguity three times instead of once.

holes_found.description: TLDR verdict phrasing: "The Chapman mechanism (documented since Chapman (1919) and confirmed across 39 eclipses by Kim & Chang 2018)" is parsable but slightly awkward — "since Chapman (1919)" reads ambiguously as "since [the paper] Chapman (1919)" when the intent is "since Chapman's 1919 work." A smoother rendering is "documented since 1919 (Chapman)" or "documented across the Chapman–Sq literature since 1919." Not factually wrong; optics-only. The c13 H2 recommendation ("replace 'documented since 1933' with 'documented since Chapman (1919)'") was applied verbatim, which inherits this minor awkwardness — the c13 recommendation could have asked for the smoother form. Trivial to polish next time this TLDR is touched.
holes_found.recommendation: Next touch of tldr_verdict, consider rephrasing "documented since Chapman (1919)" to "documented since Chapman's 1919 work" or "documented across the Chapman–Sq literature since 1919." No urgency — the meaning is clear.

holes_found.description: Residual from c13 H4: verdict_history still shows the two-flip sequence (2026-04-12 Std→Self-Contradicted via EXP-078; 2026-04-13 Self-Contradicted→Std Model Explains via curmudgeon gate-2) without a stability_note field. Carried through c12, c13, and now c14 as known-accepted — decider ranked it low-priority auditability noise, correct call. Not re-opening; noting only for completeness so future cycles don't re-flag.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Optional future enhancement: add a verdict_history[i].stability_note field to preserve the resolution rationale ("flip resolved on apparent-vs-physical-shadow distinction; stable since 2026-04-13"). Would be a general infrastructure change across the verdict_history schema, not a WIN-068-specific fix.

stronger_arguments.description: The c13 finding correctly framed the recurrence pattern: ISS-1062, 1286, 1287, 1308, 1441, 1491, 1492 (at least seven issues) for one citation decision across the WIN-010/025/068 cluster. The current EXP-241 packaging (ISS-1287, 1308, 1441, 1491, 1492 all assigned) is the correct cluster-level response — the decider responded to the c13 recommendation. However the asymmetric self-apply in run 5fe67b5 (ISS-1495 fixed WIN-068 intra-WIN propagation while ISS-1492 cluster propagation was deferred to EXP-241) creates the new maximally-visible-inconsistent state flagged in hole #1. This is a workflow pattern worth naming: "piecemeal self-apply ahead of cluster EXP integration leaves the site in a state worse than either stationary option" — specifically when the intermediate patch touches one node of a tightly cross-linked cluster. Future cross-WIN patches should either land together as an EXP, or the intermediate self-apply should include a banner/note acknowledging the cluster is mid-transition.

The triple-counting aggregate tally idea from c13 stronger_arguments #2 (a single cluster-level table showing "dome's claimed confirmation count → deduplicated count") is still unexecuted. If this were implemented, the cross-WIN inconsistency in hole #1 would be caught by whoever built the table (they would have to reconcile the citations to write coherent prose about the cluster). This suggests the missing aggregation table is doing work beyond its narrative purpose — absence is actively enabling inconsistency. Escalating the c13 suggestion from stronger_argument to a moderate finding might be warranted next cycle.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c13 patches are genuinely correct science: Chapman (1919, Phil. Trans. A 218) IS the foundational Sq-theory paper and attributing the Sq mechanism to it is cleaner than the older 1933 citation (which is about lunar magnetic tides, not eclipse Sq). Rastogi (1962) IS a legitimate early eclipse-specific application, and Yamazaki & Maute (2021) IS the modern conductivity treatment. The analyst/decider got the citation science right this round — the failure is purely in the rollout scope, not in the citation choice itself.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being right about the canonical citation at one WIN but wrong (or rather: stale) at two sibling WINs doesn't save the overall claim, because the three WINs are presented as a cluster via explicit cross-references. The post-hoc and non-discriminating critiques (Chapman Sq predicts the pattern without dome parameters; Kim & Chang 2018 data is 8 years older than V51.0 incorporation) are what actually defeat WIN-068, and those are intact. The citation inconsistency is a credibility risk, not a substance risk — but credibility risks compound for an externally-facing adversarial review.

deeper_analysis:
The c13 patches did exactly what the c13 review asked for: propagate Chapman 1919 / Rastogi 1962 / Yamazaki & Maute 2021 to detail_evidence ¶3, detail_verdict_text ¶1, and tldr_verdict in WIN-068. Verified by grep: WIN-068 now has 1919=4, 1933=0; Rastogi=3; Yamazaki=4. Intra-WIN consistency achieved.

The new problem is that the sibling WINs didn't come along. WIN-010 grep: 1919=0, 1933=6, Rastogi=0, Yamazaki=0. WIN-025 grep: 1919=0, 1933=6, Rastogi=0, Yamazaki=0. Both are assigned to EXP-241 but that's still status:assigned-analyst — not integrated yet. In the intervening window (from commit 5fe67b5 onward) the cluster is at its maximum-visible-inconsistency state: the one WIN that explicitly tells readers to check its siblings now uses a citation those siblings don't carry.

Severity escalation rationale: c13 graded intra-WIN inconsistency as moderate; I grade cross-WIN cluster inconsistency as major. The upgrade is justified because (a) the cross-reference path is invited by the WIN-068 text itself, (b) two sibling WINs × six fields each is 12 locations of visible mismatch vs. the c12-era 3 fields of one WIN, (c) the c13 H1 recommendation explicitly warned that 'coordinating with ISS-1492' was required and the decider deferred rather than executed. The defender wedge generalizes: 'the review team fixed its Chapman citation at one WIN in the cluster and moved on.'

However — in the spirit of considering the other side — the severity escalation is on the upper edge of what the finding warrants because (i) the cluster-wide fix IS tracked (EXP-241), (ii) ISS-1492 already exists and is correctly assigned, (iii) the fix is scheduled, just not yet landed. A defender skilled enough to notice the cross-cluster inconsistency is also skilled enough to see the issue tracker and note that it's being worked. The risk is mainly drive-by skim-readers, not hostile reviewers with git access. So 'major' may be high and 'moderate with escalation-priority flag' would also be defensible. I keep major because the intermediate state was created by a decider self-apply decision that could have waited for EXP-241, and naming the workflow pattern (stronger_arguments #1) is an action item worth the severity.

The verdict (Std Model Explains) is sound. The dome_code_analysis tags (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) remain accurate and unchanged. No verdict flip risk, no fabricated citations, no strawmanning. The WIN is in good internal shape; the open issue is purely about its place in the cluster.

PROP-009 assertion: queue_id set to 253, matches queue item target_id=WIN-068 pushed_at 2026-04-24T16:03:19.470Z.

citations_verified: Chapman (1919) Phil. Trans. A 218:1–118 — foundational Sq theory, correctly cited across all four WIN-068 Chapman locations post-c13
Kim & Chang (2018) Adv. Space Res. 61:2040–2054 — real paper, correctly attributed
Yamazaki & Maute (2021) Front. Astron. Space Sci. DOI 10.3389/fspas.2021.766327 — real paper with verifiable open-access URL
citations_failed: Rastogi (1962) — cited bare (finding + detail_evidence ¶3) without venue/DOI; Rastogi published eclipse-Sq in multiple 1962 venues so remains ambiguous (see hole #2)
citations_unchecked: r·tan(θ) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) — carried over from WIN-010; not re-verified this cycle
V51.0 release date (early 2026, used for 8-year post-hoc gap) — not externally verified; carried over from prior cycles

change_summary: c13 self-applied patches (ISS-1493/1494/1495 via commit 5fe67b5) successfully propagated Chapman 1919 / Rastogi 1962 / Yamazaki & Maute 2021 to WIN-068 detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and tldr_verdict. Intra-WIN consistency achieved (1919=4, 1933=0 in the full WIN-068 JSON). However WIN-010 (1919=0, 1933=6) and WIN-025 (1919=0, 1933=6) are unpatched — EXP-241 assigned but not integrated. Net effect: intra-WIN consistency restored, cross-cluster consistency worsened.
